cards.title: Stop Fixing AI Code: The Next Wave of Dev Tools Will Let *You* Train the AI
cards.thesis: While major tech companies are pouring resources into advanced AI chips and infrastructure (the hardware and software that make AI run), many everyday developers are hitting a wall. They're finding that AI-generated code is often wrong or biased, leading to more frustration and manual fixing than actual productivity gains. This creates a huge demand for AI tools that offer more control and customization, letting developers guide the AI rather than just accepting its generic output.

evidence.text: Many developers feel the code isn't the primary source of truth anymore; it's just 'ask Claude' (referring to an AI assistant).

One developer noted: 'any time I've used [AI], the output has always been off. And when the output is off I have to go and read through everything... which at that point I might as well write it myself.'

Someone asked: 'Why don't LLM harnesses enable/expose custom middleware hooks?' (meaning, why can't developers easily insert their own code to modify how AI models process information or generate output).
A builder expressed frustration with their current job, feeling they've 'outsourced our thinking, our design, our documentation, our sanity to [AI] and I just can't do it anymore.'
There's high interest in new, all-in-one developer toolkits like Nub (a Bun-like tool for Node.js), showing developers are actively seeking better, more efficient ways to build.

cards.opportunity: People are fed up with AI-generated code that's 'off' or biased, making them spend more time fixing it than if they wrote it from scratch. There's a huge opening for a tool that lets developers easily 'train' an AI assistant on their *own codebase's style and patterns* – like a custom style guide for AI – so it always generates code that fits perfectly, rather than generic, error-prone suggestions. You could build a Replit plugin that takes a project, analyzes its coding style, and then filters or refines AI suggestions to match.
